root_text.sn40.4:1.4: ‘idha bhikkhu sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā pubbeva somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā adukkhamasukhaṁ upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.sn40.4:1.6: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā pubbeva somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā adukkhamasukhaṁ upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn40.4:1.7: Tassa mayhaṁ, āvuso, iminā vihārena viharato sukhasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti.
root_text.sn40.4:2.1: Atha kho maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā iddhiyā upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.4:2.3: Mā, brāhmaṇa, catutthaṁ jhānaṁ pamādo, catutthe jhāne cittaṁ saṇṭhapehi, catutthe jhāne cittaṁ ekodiṁ karohi, catutthe jhāne cittaṁ samādahā’ti.
root_text.sn40.4:2.4: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, aparena samayena sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā pubbeva somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā adukkhamasukhaṁ upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihāsiṁ.
root_text.sn40.4:2.5: Yañhi taṁ, āvuso, sammā vadamāno vadeyya …pe… mahābhiññataṁ patto”ti.

translation_text.sn40.4:0.3: A Question About the Fourth Absorption
translation_text.sn40.4:1.1: “They speak of this thing called the ‘fourth absorption’.
translation_text.sn40.4:1.4: ‘It’s when, with the giving up of pleasure and pain and the disappearance of former happiness and sadness, a mendicant enters and remains in the fourth absorption, without pleasure or pain, with pure equanimity and mindfulness.
translation_text.sn40.4:1.5: This is called the fourth absorption.’
translation_text.sn40.4:1.6: And so … I was entering and remaining in the fourth absorption.
translation_text.sn40.4:1.7: While I was in that meditation, perception and focus accompanied by pleasure beset me.
translation_text.sn40.4:2.1: Then the Buddha came up to me with his psychic power and said,
translation_text.sn40.4:2.3: Don’t neglect the fourth absorption, brahmin! Settle your mind in the fourth absorption; unify your mind and immerse it in the fourth absorption.’
translation_text.sn40.4:2.4: And so, after some time … I entered and remained in the fourth absorption.
translation_text.sn40.4:2.5: So if anyone should be rightly called a disciple who attained to great direct knowledge with help from the Teacher, it’s me.”
